title: Does anyone here actually trade on news sentiment?
author: Fine-Perspective-438
selftext:
https://reddit.com/link/1ssm5si/video/l7gu508eyqwg1/player

In AI trading, I think information and news still matter. The AI has to 

be fed something, after all. Of course if you're a 100% pure chart 

trader, a tool like SandClaw is enough. Anything drawn on a chart can 

be captured through images or natural language.

But news is strange. Viewed from one angle it looks good, from another 

angle it looks bad. The criteria you use to read it is what really 

matters.



Personal analysis matters, but I think market analysis is more accurate 

when it's connected contextually across markets. How do you set that 

criteria? Purely by yourself? Or by reading random opinion posts on the 

internet? The standard feels really vague to me.



So I tried building a mobile app. After each market close (US, UK, 

Japan, Korea, crypto), it takes the issues and news that matter, scores 

them for sentiment, and has Gemini connect them contextually to past 

news along a time series.



Now I want to test it.



I'd like real investors from different countries to evaluate it honestly. 

16 language translation and voice TTS included.

Honestly curious how real investors would evaluate it.

comments.author: Fine-Perspective-438
comments.body:
If anyone wants to try it on Android, it's in closed beta.

No account, no sign-up, no data collection.

1. Join: [https://groups.google.com/g/eightyplus-testers](https://groups.google.com/g/eightyplus-testers)

2. Opt-in (same Gmail, on phone):

   [https://play.google.com/apps/testing/com.sandclaw.eightyplusapp](https://play.google.com/apps/testing/com.sandclaw.eightyplusapp)
